California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Judith L. Teichman 
Deputy Director 
Asian Art Museum of San Francisco 
Golden Gate Park 
San Francisco, CA 
94118 
Dear Ms. Teichman: 
March 1, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. I-89-102 
You have requested advice concerning the duties of San 
Francisco city officials under the financial disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ This letter confirms 
that your letter dated February 8, 1989, accurately summarizes the 
telephone advice I provided to you on February 2, 1989. 
Because 
you have not provided the name of the official who you are 
advising, we consider your letter to be a request for informal 
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed) .2/ 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any further 
questions regarding this matter. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
B~E~'D~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2/ 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Section 83114; Regulation Section 18329(c) (3).) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


February 8, 1989 
Kathy Donovan, Counsel 
Legal Division 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street 
Sacramento, Calif. 95814 
Re: 
Reporting Gifts of Tickets to Museum Fundraisers 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
This letter will confirm the content of my telephone conversation 
on February 2 with you and Ted Prim of the State Attorney General's 
Office. 
By way of background, the Asian Art Museum is a department of the 
City and County of San Francisco. Its governing body is appointed 
by the Mayor. 
The City provides about half of the funding for the 
Museum; the other half of the funding is raised privately through 
the Asian Art Museum Foundation, a nonprofit corporation. 
The 
Museum has scheduled a fundraising party for February 9 and offered 
a pair of complimentary tickets to the event to various City 
officials, including the Mayor, the Chief Administrative Officer 
and members of the Board of Supervisors. 
The tickets are priced 
at $150 each. 
Although we have not yet established the fair market 
value of the dinner and entertainment, 
we 
know that it is 
substantially less than the ticket price. 
One of our officials recently inquired whether he would be required 
to report the value of the tickets were he to accept the 
invitation, and, if so, what the value of the tickets is for 
Political Reform Act reporting and disqualification purposes. 
For 
the purpose of this discussion, we understand that an official is 
not disqualified from making a decision affecting the Museum unless 
the official receives from the Museum reportable benefits with a 
fair market value of $250 or more in a 12 month period. 
Based on our discussion, I have advised our local official that if 
the tickets are used by him and his spouse or another member of his 
immediate family, he need not report the value of the tickets 
because the Museum is a City-owned facility and he is an officer 
of the City. 
11 


Kathy Donovan - Tickets to Museum Fundraisers -
2/8/89 
In the future, when we offer City officials complimentary tickets 
to our fundraisers, we will advise them that they need not report 
the value of the tickets for purposes of the Political Reform Act 
unless the tickets are used by someone other than the official, or 
the official's spouse or a member of the immediate family. 
since there will be some officials who will want to bring a guest, 
we will include in the invitation our best estimate of the fair 
market value of the dinner or other benefit received by those who 
attend the 
event 
and 
advise that there are 
reporting 
and 
disqualification requirements if the official gives the tickets to 
third parties to use. 
Thank you for your assistance in sorting these issues. 
Sincerely, 
L. Teichman 
Deputy Director 
cc: 
Ted Prim 
Burk Delventhal/Randy Riddle 
Kathryn Pennypacker
